markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games has delayed Grand Theft Auto VI twice in 2025, first from a Fall 2025 window to May 26, 2026, and then again to November 19, 2026, citing the need for additional polish on the open-world title set in Vice City. These official announcements from the studio and parent company Take-Two have shifted industry focus toward quality assurance rather than rushed launches, influencing release schedules across competing titles and highlighting historical patterns where Rockstar prioritizes refinement. Traders monitoring the market should track any new trailers, marketing pushes, or further statements from Rockstar, as the extended timeline creates more runway for unexpected developments before the November 2026 launch.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The recent mistrial in Harvey Weinstein’s third New York rape retrial on May 15, 2026, after jurors deadlocked on the Jessica Mann charge, has sharply elevated the implied probability of no additional prison time to 79.3%. Prosecutors now face a June 24 hearing to decide on a potential fourth trial, while Weinstein, age 74 and in declining health at Rikers, continues serving a 16-year California sentence from his 2022 conviction and awaits sentencing on a separate 2025 New York count carrying up to 25 years. Ongoing appeals, his long incarceration since 2020, and reported plea discussions further support trader consensus that further convictions remain uncertain amid the entertainment industry’s long-running reckoning with his case.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vegas Golden Knights hold a 2-1 lead in the 2026 Stanley Cup Final after back-to-back overtime and double-overtime road victories, positioning them as the trader favorite at 63.7% implied probability. Their path featured a sweep of the Presidents' Trophy-winning Colorado Avalanche in the Western Conference Final, bolstered by strong defensive play and goaltending stability. Carolina Hurricanes, at 36.5%, advanced with dominant Eastern Conference performances but face the challenge of overcoming the series deficit on the road. Recent game outcomes have shifted market consensus toward the Knights, reflecting their current momentum and home-ice edge in the remaining contests.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Knicks' 2-0 lead in the NBA Finals against the San Antonio Spurs has driven overwhelming trader consensus behind them as 2026 champion favorites. New York extended its playoff winning streak to 13 games with road victories in Games 1 and 2, including a 105-104 thriller in Game 2, while showcasing strong defensive stops and contributions from Jalen Brunson and Karl-Anthony Towns. The Spurs, who posted the league's best 62-20 regular-season record behind Victor Wembanyama, dropped both opening contests despite home-court advantage and now face elimination pressure heading into Game 3 at Madison Square Garden. This recent momentum shift has positioned the Knicks with the clearest path to the title, though the Spurs retain realistic comeback potential in a best-of-seven series.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping's consolidated control over the Chinese Communist Party, military, and state institutions continues to underpin trader expectations of continuity through late 2026. Recent military leadership adjustments have been interpreted as further centralization rather than erosion of authority, while Xi has maintained a visible role in directing the 15th Five-Year Plan preparations and conducting high-level diplomacy, including a May 2026 summit in Beijing. No substantiated health incidents, elite factional challenges, or succession announcements have emerged to alter the trajectory ahead of the 2027 Party Congress. Analysts highlight the absence of a designated successor and the structural barriers to abrupt removal under current CCP norms, with any shift requiring unprecedented internal upheaval that has not materialized.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads trader consensus at 25% for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination because his role as California governor has given him sustained national visibility through public criticism of the Trump administration, media appearances, and early positioning ahead of the 2026 midterms. No candidates have formally declared, leaving a fragmented field where Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez draws support at 9% through progressive activism and travel, while Kamala Harris and Jon Ossoff follow at lower shares reflecting their prior national exposure. Key differentiators include governors' records on state-level issues versus congressional profiles, perceived general-election appeal in swing states, and ability to consolidate party factions. Midterm results, endorsements from key blocs, and early fundraising could narrow the field before primaries begin.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads trader consensus at 25% for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination because his role as California governor has given him sustained national visibility through public criticism of the Trump administration, media appearances, and early positioning ahead of the 2026 midterms. No candidates have formally declared, leaving a fragmented field where Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez draws support at 9% through progressive activism and travel, while Kamala Harris and Jon Ossoff follow at lower shares reflecting their prior national exposure. Key differentiators include governors' records on state-level issues versus congressional profiles, perceived general-election appeal in swing states, and ability to consolidate party factions. Midterm results, endorsements from key blocs, and early fundraising could narrow the field before primaries begin.
